title: Vermont Judiciary multiple live third-party listings remain exposed through a public listing surface
incident_classification: live judiciary listing and identity-association risk
known_facts: The identified Vermont Judiciary page functions as a live public listing surface for unpublished entry orders.
The source page contains multiple listings associated with the name strings Justin Ames Gamache and Justin Gamache.
The listings appear on a judiciary-hosted public page outside user control.
The exposure pattern is not limited to a single listing event but reflects multiple matter titles on one source surface.
The source page should be treated as a source-level discovery surface rather than as a single isolated document.
These listings are live public judiciary listings rather than stale dead-link artifacts, broken routes, or removed-site remnants.

listed_matters.caption: Justin Ames Gamache v. State of Vermont
Justin Ames Gamache v. Alexander Burke
Justin Ames Gamache v. Thomas Mozzer

what_is_not_established: This incident record does not by itself establish the search-index status of every subordinate listing or linked entry-order page.
This incident record does not by itself establish whether each listed matter is independently surfaced by external search engines.
This incident record does not by itself establish broader technical attribution beyond the public judiciary source surface directly identified.
This incident record does not convert the judiciary-hosted source page into a user-controlled surface.

impact_assessment.summary: The continued availability of multiple judiciary-hosted listings on a single public source page creates a cumulative discovery condition that can reinforce title-level identity association, source-authority exposure, and persistent public routing through an authoritative third-party listing surface.

desired_end_state: Reduce unnecessary amplification of these listings where possible through broader canonical and suppression strategy.
Preserve technical documentation that these are live third-party public judiciary listings rather than stale dead routes.
Maintain classification separating these listings from stale-result incidents involving dead URLs or removed personal sites.
root_cause_assessment: These listings appear to be active public judiciary listings maintained by a third-party source outside user control. The relevant issue is ongoing public discoverability of live records through an authoritative source page rather than stale indexing of removed or broken pages.
governance_rationale: Because the identified source page functions as a live judiciary-hosted public listing surface containing multiple matter listings associated with the target name strings, it should be treated as an active non-academic association source requiring restrictive governance treatment rather than as a stale-result or dead-route incident.
recommended_governance_treatment: Treat this as a live judiciary listing-surface incident.
Track the source page as a multi-listing public discovery surface.
Distinguish this incident from dead-route, stale-result, and removed-site incident classes.
Preserve separate notation for each listed matter while maintaining one source-level incident family.
actions_taken: Classified the listings separately from stale dead-route incidents.
pending_actions: Evaluate whether any search-layer suppression is available.

evidence.listing_structure: multiple matter titles associated with Justin Ames Gamache or Justin Gamache
